  "description": "Expedier is the pioneering Black-led BIPOC Global Money App, designed to empower Africans in the diaspora with accessible financial solutions. Our app enables users to efficiently manage multiple currency wallets, pay bills, and navigate their finances across borders. We are committed to making financial success attainable for immigrants, whether they are at home or abroad.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: FX Sales Agent

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The Role


	We are seeking a seasoned FX Sales Specialist with a minimum of 5 years of experience in the foreign exchange or financial services sector.
	You aren&#39;t just a salesperson; you are a strategic partner to your clients, navigating the complexities of global markets to deliver value.


FX Sales Agent Target:


	Experienced professionals looking for stability plus high-growth incentives. Headline: Drive the Market. Own the Relationship. Earn the Reward.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and grow a portfolio of high-net-worth (HNW) and corporate clients. &bull; Execute FX transactions with precision and deep market understanding.
	Identify new business opportunities and convert leads into long-term partners.


Requirements


	Experience: Minimum 5 years in FX Sales, Treasury, or Investment Banking.
	Network: A proven track record and an existing book of business are highly preferred.
	Skills: Exceptional negotiation, analytical thinking, and a &quot;closer&quot; mindset.


Why Join Us?


	Stable Base: A competitive monthly salary of N450,000 + Aggressive Commission Structure.
	Profit Sharing: Earn a massive 30% commission on the company&rsquo;s profit margin for your trades.
	Growth: Work in a fast-paced environment where your impact is directly rewarded.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;hr@expedier.co&nbsp;using &quot;FX Sales Agent Application&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Campus Operations & Customer Success Lead",
  "description": "Spacefinish is a global design and workspace strategy firm specializing in the design, build, and transformation of office spaces. We help organizations create smart, functional, and future-ready environments that drive productivity, collaboration, and business growth. Trusted by leading companies and fast-growing startups, we combine design thinking, technology, and execution excellence to deliver world-class workspaces tailored to modern work needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Campus Operations &amp; Customer Success Lead

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Campus Operations &amp; Experience Lead is responsible for the day-to-day performance of Campus locations, ensuring each space operates smoothly, delivers a high-quality client experience, and meets occupancy and profitability targets.
	This role combines operations management and client experience ownership, with a strong focus on systems, efficiency, and retention. The successful candidate will run each location as a high-performing unit, reducing operational friction and ensuring clients stay, renew, and grow within Campus.


Key Responsibilities
Location Operations Management:


	Oversee daily operations across all Campus locations
	Ensure all facilities (power, internet, cleaning, security) are fully functional at all times
	Manage and coordinate vendors to meet agreed service levels
	Identify and resolve operational issues promptly, preventing recurrence


Client Experience &amp; Retention:


	Manage the full client lifecycle from onboarding to renewal
	Ensure a seamless move-in experience for all new clients
	Conduct regular client check-ins to maintain satisfaction and identify risks early
	Resolve client issues efficiently and professionally
	Drive client retention, renewals, and expansion within locations


New Location Setup &amp; Launch:


	Support the operational setup of new locations
	Coordinate vendors and internal teams to ensure readiness for launch
	Implement standardized operating procedures from day one
	Ensure new locations meet Campus experience and performance standards


Systems, Reporting &amp; Compliance:


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date records on: occupancy, client status, issue logs, operational performance
	Ensure adherence to all operational processes and systems
	Prepare weekly performance reports covering: occupancy, client feedback, key issues, cost, and efficiency metrics.


Unit Economics &amp; Performance Management:


	Track and manage key performance indicators, including: occupancy rates, revenue per desk, and cost per desk
	Identify inefficiencies and implement cost control measures
	Support pricing and space utilization decisions through data insights


Cross-Functional Coordination:


	Work closely with: Sales team, Design team for space setup and modifications, Finance team (billing, collections, cost tracking)
	Ensure alignment between sales commitments and operational delivery


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field
	4&ndash;8+ years in operations roles within: serviced offices/coworking, hospitality (hotels), property or facility management
	Proven experience managing end-to-end operational processes and improving efficiency
	Strong background in customer experience management, with a focus on client satisfaction and retention
	Demonstrated ability to build systems, lead, mentor, and manage teams effectively
	Experience working in fast-paced, structured environments, with the ability to handle multiple priorities.


Salary
N400,000 - N600,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;hiring@spacefinish.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the susbject of the email.",

	Strong team leadership and cross-functional collaboration skills, with the ability to elevate both execution standards and creative quality.
	Highly trend-sensitive, analytical, and capable of turning data and insights into actionable improvements.

  "description": "Independent Project Monitoring Company Limited (IPMC) is one of Nigeria oilfield service companies providing innovative solutions, technology, and services to the oil and gas industry.&nbsp;The Company operates in Nigeria and has a network of, manufacturing, service, research and development, and training facilities.

It delivers innovative technologies and services designed to meet the world&#39;s current and future energy needs in a safe, ethical, and sustainable manner. Grounded by our core values and inspired by our world-class people, we are committed to being a trusted business partner to those we serve.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Lead Civil &amp; Costing Consulting Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Position Overview&nbsp;


	As Lead Civil &amp; Costing Consulting Engineer, you will head our civil-engineering discipline and costing practice within an independent third-party valuation and certification firm.
	You&rsquo;ll oversee structural/civil design reviews, site inspections, cost plans, valuations and certification reports&mdash;ensuring asset integrity, regulatory compliance and cost transparency for lenders, insurers, developers and public-sector clients.&nbsp;


Key Responsibilities&nbsp;
Technical Leadership &amp; Team Management:


	Lead, mentor and develop a multidisciplinary team of civil engineers, quantity surveyors and cost analysts.&nbsp;
	Establish standardised procedures for design review, cost estimation, valuation and certification.&nbsp;


Civil Engineering Review &amp; Certification:


	Review civil / structural drawings, specifications and calculations for compliance with applicable codes (e.g., Eurocodes, ACI, AASHTO, local regulations).&nbsp;
	Conduct site visits and inspections (earthworks, foundations, drainage, pavements, retaining structures).&nbsp;
	Identify defects, design risks and non-conformances; recommend remedial actions.&nbsp;
	Prepare certification reports for asset handover, milestone acceptance and regulatory compliance.&nbsp;


Cost Estimating &amp; Valuation:


	Lead preparation of elemental cost plans, detailed quantity take-offs and life-cycle cost analyses.&nbsp;
	Perform market-aligned valuations using recognised standards (IVS, EVS, RICS Red Book).&nbsp;
	Advise on budget programming, risk allowances and cost-optimisation strategies.&nbsp;
	Support due-diligence assignments for financing, M&amp;A, refinancing and insurance renewals.&nbsp;


Client Reporting &amp; Interface:


	Produce clear, concise technical memoranda, valuation certificates and executive summaries.&nbsp;
	Present findings, cost opinions and certification results to clients, lenders and stakeholders.&nbsp;
	Act as the firm&rsquo;s independent representative during client meetings, project hand-overs and audits.&nbsp;


Project &amp; Quality Management:


	Define scopes of work, resource plans and project schedules for civil and costing assignments.&nbsp;
	Monitor project budgets, deliverables and turnaround times; ensure on-time, on-budget delivery.&nbsp;
	Uphold quality management standards (e.g., ISO 9001) and internal audit processes.&nbsp;


Required Qualifications&nbsp;
Education &amp; Professional Status:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s or Master&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering or Quantity Surveying.&nbsp;
	Chartered / registered status (CEng, MRICS, CIOB or equivalent).&nbsp;


&nbsp;Experience:


	10+ years in civil/structural engineering design, inspection or certification.&nbsp;
	5+ years leading cost planning, quantity surveying or independent valuation teams.&nbsp;


Technical Skills:


	In-depth knowledge of civil engineering codes, geotechnical principles and construction methods.&nbsp;
	Proven expertise in cost-estimating software (e.g., CostX, BCIS, Timberline) and MS Office.&nbsp;
	Familiarity with valuation methodologies under global standards (IVS 300 series, EVS).&nbsp;


&nbsp;Soft Skills:


	Excellent report-writing and presentation abilities.&nbsp;
	Strong leadership, client-relationship management and negotiation skills.&nbsp;
	Analytical mindset with rigorous attention to detail.&nbsp;


Desirable Attributes:


	Experience in sectors such as infrastructure, transportation, utilities, ports/harbours or heavy industry.&nbsp;
	Familiarity with BIM/CAD tools (Revit, AutoCAD Civil 3D).&nbsp;
	Certification in project management (PMP, PRINCE2) or quality systems (ISO 17020).&nbsp;

Lead Civil & Costing Consulting Engineer Job at Independent Project Monitoring Company Limited




        
    

        
        Independent Project Monitoring Company Limited (IPMC) is one of Nigeria oilfield service companies providing innovative solutions, technology, and services to the oil and gas industry. The Company operates in Nigeria and has a network of, manufacturing, service, research and development, and training facilities.

It delivers innovative technologies and services designed to meet the world's current and future energy needs in a safe, ethical, and sustainable manner. Grounded by our core values and inspired by our world-class people, we are committed to being a trusted business partner to those we serve.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Lead Civil & Costing Consulting Engineer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Position Overview 


	As Lead Civil & Costing Consulting Engineer, you will head our civil-engineering discipline and costing practice within an independent third-party valuation and certification firm.
	You’ll oversee structural/civil design reviews, site inspections, cost plans, valuations and certification reports—ensuring asset integrity, regulatory compliance and cost transparency for lenders, insurers, developers and public-sector clients. 


Key Responsibilities 
Technical Leadership & Team Management:


	Lead, mentor and develop a multidisciplinary team of civil engineers, quantity surveyors and cost analysts. 
	Establish standardised procedures for design review, cost estimation, valuation and certification. 


Civil Engineering Review & Certification:


	Review civil / structural drawings, specifications and calculations for compliance with applicable codes (e.g., Eurocodes, ACI, AASHTO, local regulations). 
	Conduct site visits and inspections (earthworks, foundations, drainage, pavements, retaining structures). 
	Identify defects, design risks and non-conformances; recommend remedial actions. 
	Prepare certification reports for asset handover, milestone acceptance and regulatory compliance. 


Cost Estimating & Valuation:


	Lead preparation of elemental cost plans, detailed quantity take-offs and life-cycle cost analyses. 
	Perform market-aligned valuations using recognised standards (IVS, EVS, RICS Red Book). 
	Advise on budget programming, risk allowances and cost-optimisation strategies. 
	Support due-diligence assignments for financing, M&A, refinancing and insurance renewals. 


Client Reporting & Interface:


	Produce clear, concise technical memoranda, valuation certificates and executive summaries. 
	Present findings, cost opinions and certification results to clients, lenders and stakeholders. 
	Act as the firm’s independent representative during client meetings, project hand-overs and audits. 


Project & Quality Management:


	Define scopes of work, resource plans and project schedules for civil and costing assignments. 
	Monitor project budgets, deliverables and turnaround times; ensure on-time, on-budget delivery. 
	Uphold quality management standards (e.g., ISO 9001) and internal audit processes. 


Required Qualifications 
Education & Professional Status:


	Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Civil Engineering or Quantity Surveying. 
	Chartered / registered status (CEng, MRICS, CIOB or equivalent). 


 Experience:


	10+ years in civil/structural engineering design, inspection or certification. 
	5+ years leading cost planning, quantity surveying or independent valuation teams. 


Technical Skills:


	In-depth knowledge of civil engineering codes, geotechnical principles and construction methods. 
	Proven expertise in cost-estimating software (e.g., CostX, BCIS, Timberline) and MS Office. 
	Familiarity with valuation methodologies under global standards (IVS 300 series, EVS). 


 Soft Skills:


	Excellent report-writing and presentation abilities. 
	Strong leadership, client-relationship management and negotiation skills. 
	Analytical mindset with rigorous attention to detail. 


Desirable Attributes:


	Experience in sectors such as infrastructure, transportation, utilities, ports/harbours or heavy industry. 
	Familiarity with BIM/CAD tools (Revit, AutoCAD Civil 3D). 
	Certification in project management (PMP, PRINCE2) or quality systems (ISO 17020). 


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Resume and a Cover letter detailing their relevant experience and qualifications to: [email protected] and copy: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
